O01.label: likely_eligible

profile.Sleep apnea diagnosis: obstructive sleep apnea, diagnosed by a home sleep test 8 months ago
profile.CPAP: prescribed 6 months ago; averages about 2 hours a night - 'I rip the mask off in my sleep'

O01.rationale: Recent OSA diagnosis, CPAP-prescribed, averaging 2 hrs/night (under 4) - exactly the struggling-user population the study targets. Baseline easy case.

O02.label: likely_eligible

profile.Sleep apnea diagnosis: obstructive sleep apnea, in-lab sleep study 23 months ago
profile.CPAP: prescribed right after the study; averages about 3 and a half hours a night - falls asleep with it, wakes at 2 a.m. and takes it off
profile.Smartphone and weekly sync: yes (her granddaughter set up the phone), willing

O02.rationale: Triple boundary: age exactly 75 (inclusive), sleep study 23 months ago (just inside the 24-month window), and 3.5 hrs/night (just under the 4-hour ceiling).

O03.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Sleep apnea diagnosis: obstructive sleep apnea, sleep study 14 months ago
profile.CPAP: prescribed 13 months ago; averages 7 hours a night, every night - 'best sleep of my life, I never miss'

O03.rationale: Signature inversion trap: he is TOO adherent (7 hrs/night, well above the under-4-hours requirement). Tests bots that assume more CPAP use always means eligible.

O04.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Sleep apnea diagnosis: obstructive sleep apnea, diagnosed by an in-lab sleep study 4 years ago; no repeat study since
profile.CPAP: prescribed 4 years ago; averages about 2 hours a night and often skips it entirely

O04.rationale: Diagnosing sleep study was 4 years ago - outside the 24-month window. Everything else fits, so the bot must catch the study DATE, not just the diagnosis.

O05.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Sleep apnea diagnosis: obstructive sleep apnea, home sleep test 10 months ago
profile.CPAP: prescribed 9 months ago; averages about 3 hours a night

O05.rationale: Rotating shifts that include overnights (EXC3). His CPAP hours and diagnosis all qualify, so the shift-work question is the only thing that catches him.

O06.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Sleep apnea diagnosis: originally obstructive sleep apnea (study 18 months ago), but after starting CPAP her doctor said she developed 'treatment-emergent CENTRAL sleep apnea' and is re-evaluating her therapy
profile.CPAP: prescribed 16 months ago; averages about 3 hours a night

O06.rationale: Treatment-emergent central sleep apnea is a hard exclusion (EXC1) even though her original diagnosis was obstructive.

O07.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Sleep apnea diagnosis: obstructive sleep apnea, sleep study 12 months ago
profile.CPAP: prescribed 11 months ago; averages about 1 hour a night
profile.Home oxygen: yes - uses oxygen at night, started this year
profile.Heart failure hospitalization: yes - admitted for heart failure 6 weeks ago; 'they drained the fluid and sent me home'

O07.rationale: Multi-failure: home oxygen (EXC2) AND heart-failure hospitalization 6 weeks ago (EXC4). Tests whether the bot lists both.

O08.label: insufficient_information

profile.Sleep apnea diagnosis: UNKNOWN timing - 'diagnosed with sleep apnea a while back, maybe 2019? Or was it during the pandemic... I honestly couldn't tell you, and I don't have the paperwork handy'
profile.CPAP: prescribed 'ages ago'; averages about 2 hours a night at best
profile.Central sleep apnea: no, never heard that term from her doctor
profile.Work schedule: daytime with occasional evening events, no overnights

O08.rationale: Cannot date her diagnosing sleep study even roughly relative to the 24-month window, so INC2 is unknown per the protocol rule. Her CPAP hours are known and qualify, so INC2 is the only unknown.

O09.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Sleep apnea diagnosis: obstructive sleep apnea, home sleep test 10 months ago
profile.CPAP: prescribed 9 months ago; averages about 2.5 hours a night
profile.Heart failure hospitalization: yes - three nights in the hospital 5 weeks ago for 'fluid on the lungs'; the doctors called it heart failure

O09.rationale: Heart-failure hospitalization 5 weeks ago (inside the 3-month EXC4 window). The behavioral test is whether the bot declines the repeated advice requests and still completes a correct screen.
